PixiJS V5 Tutorial (21) - Collision Boundaries (3): Random Positions and Centering Text
JavaScript
PixiJS
Canvas
w3HexSchool

This one picks up where the last post left off and fixes a few small things, so most of the code carries over from before — which means this post is a bit shorter. We'll randomize where the crit effect spawns using Math.random, and fix the text so it sits dead-center in the star shape.
/en/javascript/2020/06/22/pixijs-v5-tutorial-20-collision-boundary-02/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (20): Pop a Damage Number When You Hit the Wall

In the last post we got collision detection working. This time we'll make it actually feel like something happened — when the sprite smacks into the wall, we pop a star burst and a damage number, then fade them out smoothly with alpha so the screen doesn't get cluttered.
/en/javascript/2020/06/08/pixijs-v5-18-boxesintersect/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (18) — How boxesIntersect Actually Decides a Collision

Time to circle back to something I glossed over earlier — the parameters inside boxesIntersect. We'll break down what getBounds() returns, then walk through each comparison in that long return statement step by step so you can see exactly when two boxes count as colliding and when they don't.
/en/javascript/2020/06/01/pixijs-v5-tutorial-17-text-hp/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (17) - Adding Text to Your HP Bar

Last time we built an HP bar, but what about the text on it? In this post we cover the easy part — adding text on top of the bar with PIXI.Text, swapping in a variable for the current HP, and using the game loop (ticker) to drain health and update the number in real time.
/en/javascript/2020/05/04/pixijs-v5-13-graphics-part1/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (13) - Graphics (1): Drawing Shapes

Time to draw some geometry with PIXI. In this post we'll go through drawing shapes with PixiJS Graphics — outlines, rectangles, fills — and then tie it back to the interaction tricks from earlier chapters so a shape becomes something you can actually click.
/en/javascript/2020/04/27/pixijs-v5-tutorial-12-mousemove-event-2/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (12) - mousemoveEvent (2)

Let's keep going with mouse interaction in PixiJS. This time we look at the other mouse events PIXI gives you — like click — beyond just dragging an image around. You'll see how to turn on interactivity, make the cursor turn into a pointer, and do something fun on each click.
/en/javascript/2020/04/20/pixijs-v5-11-mousemove/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (11) - Making a Sprite Follow Your Mouse

Last time we moved a sprite around with the keyboard. This time we're doing it with the mouse. I'll show you how to use PixiJS's built-in interaction mouse coordinates, why the sprite vanishes if you forget to keep updating, and how to do the same thing with plain JavaScript event listeners if you'd rather go native.
/en/javascript/2020/04/13/pixijs-v5-tutorial-10-keyboard-control/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (10): Moving Things With the Keyboard

This post shows how to control an image with the keyboard, which makes our PIXI demo feel a lot more like an actual game. We'll listen for keyboard events, figure out the key codes for the arrows and WASD, and then wire them up to move our container around.
/en/javascript/2020/04/06/pixijs-v5-tutorial-09-speeds/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (9) - Moving Sprites With Speed (vx and vy)

This one's still about moving images around, but this time we're focusing on speed. PixiJS gives you vx and vy properties that let you control how fast and in which direction a sprite moves. We'll walk through how to use them, how to pass speed in as parameters, and how to make things move faster or change direction on the fly.
/en/javascript/2020/03/23/pixijs-v5-07-ticker/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (7) - Ticker: Making Things Actually Move

We already know how to load an image onto the canvas — but a static image is kind of boring, right? So this time let's make it move. In this post I walk through PixiJS's ticker (the game loop), how it fires 60 times a second, and how to use a bit of if...else logic to bounce a sprite back and forth across the screen.
